setting.note: Almost the whole novel is held inside one grand bourgeois apartment building on the Left Bank, at 7 rue de Grenelle. A concierge who hides her learning, a wealthy set of tenants, and a girl planning her own disappearance share its lodge, stairwell and flats, with a café and the surrounding streets of the 7th arrondissement just beyond the door.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Muriel Barbery, L'Élégance du hérisson, first published 2006. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Elegance of the Hedgehog, Muriel Barbery (2006). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A muted aubergine, the tone of the camellias and quiet refinement the concierge keeps hidden. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
